candidates.row_label: Depreciation, amortization and impairment of non-current assets
candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line above financial items, so it does not appear embedded in the selected COGS row. However, it is a company-level total that also includes impairment and is not identified as COGS-specific, so adding it to COGS would risk misallocation/double counting.
Operating depreciation detail line beneath the broader D&A summary, but not labelled as COGS/cost of sales and not clearly attributable to the selected COGS bucket.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation on Right of use assets
candidates.why: Operating depreciation component, but no indication it belongs specifically to COGS rather than SG&A or mixed functions.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Raw materials and consumables'. Explicit D&A exists separately at the operating level, so it is likely not embedded in that row, but the excerpt provides no COGS-specific D&A split. Conservative output avoids allocating total/mixed D&A to COGS.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation, amortization and impairment of non-current assets

But it is a total operating D&A line including impairment and is not identified as SG&A-specific, so adding it to XSGA would risk overstatement/misallocation.
Operating depreciation component beneath the summary D&A line, but not labelled as SG&A/admin/overhead specific.
Could plausibly relate to overhead functions, but there is no explicit SG&A linkage and it appears only as a component under the broader D&A section.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation on Right of use assets
candidates.why: Could relate partly to office/overhead, but no explicit SG&A designation is provided.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA bucket is the combined 'Other external costs' + 'Employee benefit expense'. Explicit D&A is presented separately from those rows, but the statement does not split D&A between COGS and SG&A and there is no R&D row. Conservative output avoids assigning mixed total D&A to XSGA without bucket-specific evidence.
